meta.notAffiliated: Independent practice tool. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the Home Office or UK Government.

_fieldGuide.id: Unique, stable ID, e.g. 'hist-0001'. Never reuse an ID once retired.
_fieldGuide.category: One of: values | uk-overview | history | society | government
_fieldGuide.subtopic: Free-text grouping for drills, e.g. 'The Tudors', 'Parliament'.
_fieldGuide.tier: 1-4 difficulty for the climb mechanic (1 = easiest, 4 = hardest).
_fieldGuide.question: Your OWN wording. Never copy a sentence from the handbook.
_fieldGuide.options: Array of answer choices shown to the user.
_fieldGuide.answer: For 'single': the index of the correct option. For 'multi': an array of indices.
_fieldGuide.explanation: Short, original explanation shown after the user answers.
_fieldGuide.fact: Internal note of the underlying fact (NOT shown to users) — helps you re-check it.
_fieldGuide.volatile: true if the answer is a figure or name that can change over time. Re-check these every cycle.
_fieldGuide.lastReviewed: ISO date this single item was last fact-checked.

questions.question: In which year did the Battle of Hastings take place?
questions.explanation: William of Normandy defeated King Harold at the Battle of Hastings in 1066, beginning Norman rule in England.

questions.question: Who is the patron saint of Scotland?
questions.explanation: St Andrew is the patron saint of Scotland, and his cross appears on the Scottish flag. (George = England, David = Wales, Patrick = Northern Ireland.)
questions.fact: Patron saints: England-George, Scotland-Andrew, Wales-David, NI-Patrick.

questions.question: How many members does the Northern Ireland Assembly have?
questions.explanation: The Northern Ireland Assembly has 90 members (MLAs) - five for each of the 18 constituencies. It was reduced from 108 in 2017.
questions.fact: NI Assembly = 90 MLAs since 2017 (previously 108). Classic example competitors get wrong.

questions.question: Which TWO of the following are British Overseas Territories?
questions.explanation: Gibraltar and the Falkland Islands are British Overseas Territories. The Republic of Ireland is an independent country, and France is unconnected to the UK.
questions.fact: British Overseas Territories include Gibraltar and the Falkland Islands.
